data.title: Sweden's Alcadon Q1 sales fall 10% EBITA margin rises
data.description:
Overview

  • Sweden network infrastructure provider's Q1 net sales fell 10% yr/yr

  • Adjusted EBITA rose 0.6% and margin improved to 7.2%

  • Operating cash flow increased to SEK 30 mln from SEK 10 mln

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific guidance for future quarters or the full year

Result Drivers

  • EXCHANGE RATE EFFECTS - Adjusted EBITA was affected by unrealised exchange rate gains and the reversal of contingent considerations

  • MARGIN IMPROVEMENT - Gross margin and adjusted EBITA margin both increased compared to the prior year

data.title: Steelmaker SSAB Notes Increasing Customer Caution Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description:
(05:30 GMT) *SSAB 1Q Sales SEK25.34B
(05:30 GMT) *SSAB: Analysts Saw 1Q Sales At SEK24.34B
(05:30 GMT) *SSAB 1Q Oper Pft SEK2.2B
(05:30 GMT) *SSAB: Analysts Saw 1Q Op Profit At SEK2.15B
(05:31 GMT) *SSAB: Limited Direct Effects on SSAB From War in Persian Gulf, But Customers More Cautious
(05:32 GMT) *SSAB: There is a Risk That a Prolonged Conflict Could Negatively Affect Economic Activity
(05:33 GMT) *SSAB Still Expects 2026 Total Maintenance Costs at SEK1.45B
(05:33 GMT) *SSAB Stil Expects 2026 Total Maintenance and Strategic Capex at Around SEK13.5B
(05:34 GMT) *SSAB 1Q Net Pft SEK1.6B
(05:34 GMT) *SSAB: Analysts Saw 1Q Net Profit At SEK1.65B
(05:36 GMT) *SSAB Sees Stable Shipments, Somehwat Higher Realized Prices in 2Q on Quarter
(06:01 GMT) Steelmaker SSAB Notes Increasing Customer Caution Amid Middle East Conflict

By Dominic Chopping

STOCKHOLM--SSAB said the direct impact on the company from the Middle East war has been limited, but warned that customers are becoming more cautious across several markets while a prolonged conflict risks hurting economic activity.

The Swedish steelmaker said that high-strength steel markets in the Middle East were impacted by the hostilities during the first quarter, while underlying demand in Europe showed some improvement and overall global markets showed some seasonally higher demand.

The European market for standard steels remained cautious during the first quarter and import volumes declined, while some production capacity among European steel producers was idled during the quarter, partly due to maintenance.

SSAB's production and shipments were higher compared to the first quarter of 2025, and for the second quarter it expects overall stable shipments of steel across its divisions with somewhat higher realized prices.

The group still expects total maintenance costs for 2026 at 1.45 billion Swedish kronor ($157.3 million) with total maintenance and strategic capital expenditure at around 13.5 billion kronor.

It posted a first-quarter net profit of 1.6 billion kronor from 1.13 billion kronor a year earlier as revenue fell 0.7% to 25.34 billion kronor.

Analysts polled by FactSet had expected net profit of 1.65 billion kronor on sales of 24.34 billion kronor.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com
